sections.paragraph:
ἐνάλιος, ον (Pind. et al.; as εἰνάλιος as early as Homer, also Philo, Decal. 54) belonging to the sea (τὰ) ἐνάλια sea creatures Js 3:7.—DELG s.v. ἅλς.

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 330
